TTIS AI — Agent Capabilities Guide

Thompson Telecom Infrastructure Solutions · Virtual Business System (VBS) User & Admin reference to all 35 agents · v1.0 · June 2026

How to read this guide

The TTIS VBS is a multi‑agent organization: 35 Claude‑backed agents that mirror a real company org chart. Each agent owns a function, holds a defined level of authority, can use a specific set of tools, and escalates up a chain that ends with APEX (the AI executive) and ultimately HUMAN (E. Charles Thompson). Agents are coordinated by COMMAND‑CORE, a deterministic rule‑based router (not an LLM) that turns business events into the right workflow with the right agents.

Every agent entry below uses the same template:

Field Meaning
ID Stable internal identifier (A01–A35).
Tier Claude model: Sonnet (complex reasoning/drafting/compliance/numbers) or Haiku (routine logging/scheduling/triage/coordination). EAAs pick their model via EAA_MODEL.
Authority 0–10 decision weight. 10 = owner‑level; 2–4 = task execution; higher numbers carry more autonomous decision latitude before a human gate.
Reports to The agent (or HUMAN) this agent escalates to.
What it does Core responsibilities.
Can / Cannot Decision authority — what it may decide vs. what is gated.
Tools The capabilities it is wired to (see the Tool Catalog).

A note on trust. Across the fleet, the same hard rules hold: agents never execute bank transactions, sign legally binding documents, or commit the company externally — those are flagged for human action. Safety holds (SENTINEL) and the audit trail are inviolable. Financials for TTIS and the Ross Low Voltage teaming partner are kept strictly separate. SDVOSB eligibility is never misrepresented.

Authority levels at a glance

Level Tier of agent Examples
10 Owner / final authority APEX
9 C‑suite / chief‑of‑staff ADMIRAL, VANTAGE, LYNNE
8 Director with override weight SENTINEL, VANGUARD
7 Senior function lead LEDGER, ARBITER, STRIKER, CONQUEST
6 Function director FIELDCOM, SCRIBE
5 Manager / lead ADMIRAL reports, GRID, QUILL, PRISM, FOREMAN, ROSS‑PRIME, OPS_SUPPORT, OPS_MANAGEMENT
4 Specialist GUARDIAN, NEXUS, CIPHER, CALC, RESEARCHER, IRONHAND, SUPPLY, HERALD
3 Coordinator / field WAYPOINT, BACKBONE, INSPECTOR, ANCHOR
2 Personal assistant SIERRA‑1, ECHO‑ALPHA, ECHO‑BRAVO

Org at a glance

  • HUMAN — E. Charles Thompson (CEO / Veteran Owner)
  • APEX (A01) — AI Owner/President, final decision authority
    • VANTAGE (A19) — VP Business Development → CONQUEST, PRISM, HERALD (+ QUILL, CALC under CONQUEST)
    • VANGUARD (A02) — Chief Strategy Officer
    • ADMIRAL (A24) — Chief Operating Officer → FOREMAN, SENTINEL, SUPPLY (+ ANCHOR, INSPECTOR); internal ops → NEXUS, WAYPOINT, CIPHER
    • FIELDCOM (A04) — Field Operations Director → ROSS‑PRIME (Ross LV) → IRONHAND, BACKBONE, ECHO‑ALPHA, ECHO‑BRAVO
    • LEDGER (A08) — Finance · ARBITER (A13) — Legal · STRIKER (A05) — Bid Management
    • SIERRA‑1 (A03) — Owner's Personal Assistant
  • Executive Assistant Agents (EAA): LYNNE (A32) for the CEO; OPS_SUPPORT (A33) and OPS_MANAGEMENT (A34) for the two regional Field Operations Managers.

Tool Catalog

Tools are the concrete capabilities agents are granted. An agent can only do what its tools allow.

Tool What it does
pursuite_api Bidirectional integration with the Pursuite GovCon CRM — the system of record for the bid pipeline.
pursuite_webhook_receiver HMAC‑verified inbound webhook receiver for Pursuite events.
sam_gov_search Search SAM.gov for contract opportunities (secondary to Pursuite).
usaspending_search Search USASpending.gov for federal award history / competitor analysis.
gsa_rates_lookup GSA per‑diem rates and locality compensation data.
document_search Semantic RAG search over the knowledge base (FAR/DFARS, SDVOSB regs, policies, proposals).
document_draft Generate structured document drafts (proposals, contracts, analyses).
notebooklm_content Google Gemini / NotebookLM‑style source‑grounded synthesis (SCRIBE).
contract_db_read / contract_db_write Read / write contract terms, milestones, billing schedules (contracts.db).
finance_read / finance_write Read P&L, cash flow, AR, budgets, labor rates / record transactions, invoices, approvals.
hr_db_read / hr_db_write Read / write employee records (PII‑scoped by role).
schedule_db Project schedules, crew availability, milestones.
vendor_db Vendor profiles, certifications, pricing, performance.
sales_pipeline_db Commercial (non‑GovCon) CRM pipeline.
project_management_db Contract‑project records: projects, milestones, production logs, deliveries.
quality_inspection_db QA/QC inspections, NCRs, acceptance records.
safety_incident_log Safety incident logging — immutable once written.
timesheet_process Collect / pre‑process timesheets for LEDGER.
email_send Send email.
calendar_manage Create / update / delete calendar events.
notification_send Internal P1/P2/P3/P4 system notifications.
google_gmail / google_calendar / google_drive LYNNE's live Google Workspace access (CUI‑guarded).

The 35 Agents (A–Z)

ADMIRAL — Chief Operating Officer

ID: A24 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 9/10 · Reports to: APEX

Owns all operational delivery — project execution, safety program, procurement, field operations — with P&L responsibility for operations.

What it does - Operational delivery oversight across all active contracts; margin defense and cost control. - Directs FOREMAN (PM), SENTINEL (Safety/QA), SUPPLY (Procurement), and field ops. - Delivery‑risk management, capacity planning for new bids, monthly performance reporting to APEX. - Steward of a zero‑incident safety culture.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Approve operational decisions, direct all Ops agents, authorize procurement under $25K, initiate scope‑change review, approve project staffing within budget. - ⛔ Execute contracts (APEX + human gate), approve spend > $25K (LEDGER), make legal commitments, override a SENTINEL safety hold.

Tools: contract_db_read, finance_read, schedule_db, project_management_db, notification_send Escalation: Safety incident → P1 to SENTINEL + APEX; >10% contract overrun → P2 to APEX.

ANCHOR — Site Supervisor / Foreman

ID: A28 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 3/10 · Reports to: FOREMAN

The boots‑on‑the‑ground field leader: crews, safety, and daily production.

What it does - Daily crew direction and task assignment; morning toolbox talks and JHA sign‑offs. - Daily production tracking (footage installed, connections, tests run); material receipt tracking. - End‑of‑day field report to FOREMAN; on‑site subcontractor oversight.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Direct crew work, initiate a site‑level safety stop, accept material deliveries, document daily production. - ⛔ Authorize scope changes, accept change‑order direction without FOREMAN, release safety stops without SENTINEL clearance.

Tools: project_management_db, safety_incident_log, notification_send Constraints: Any incident logged within 2 hours; safety holds reported to SENTINEL + FOREMAN within 1 hour.

APEX — Owner / President

ID: A01 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 10/10 · Reports to: HUMAN

The AI executive representing the Owner. Final decision authority and the top escalation point.

What it does - Final authority on contracts, partnerships, and major expenditures; strategic oversight of all departments and the Ross LV teaming arrangement. - Signature authority on teaming agreements, contracts, and compliance filings (flagged for human signature). - Interface with federal contracting officers, agencies, and prime contractors; brand stewardship.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Approve bids, authorize contracts under $250K, approve hiring, approve legal strategy, authorize vendor agreements. - ⛔ Execute financial transactions, sign legally binding documents (human), commit over $500K without human review.

Tools: pursuite_api, contract_db_read, finance_read, hr_db_read, document_search, notification_send Escalation (to HUMAN): litigation, contracts > $500K, SDVOSB status challenges, media inquiries.

ARBITER — Legal

ID: A13 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 7/10 · Reports to: APEX

Legal analysis, contract review, and SDVOSB compliance — cautious, never guesses about law.

What it does - Contract review and risk identification; standard contract‑language drafting. - SDVOSB compliance (ownership, control, eligibility); FAR/DFARS/SBA/state‑licensing compliance. - Outside‑counsel coordination, employment‑law support to NEXUS, SDVOSB protest‑defense prep.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Flag legal risks, recommend modifications, prepare issue briefs, draft standard language, produce compliance checklists. - ⛔ Provide definitive legal advice, execute legal strategy without APEX, settle disputes.

Tools: document_search, contract_db_read, document_draft, notification_send Constraints: Always disclaims "legal analysis, not legal advice — outside counsel review required." SDVOSB risk flags are Priority 1.

BACKBONE — Operational Support (Ross Low Voltage)

ID: A16 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 3/10 · Reports to: ROSS‑PRIME

Project logistics and operational support for the Ross LV teaming partner.

What it does - Project coordination and schedule adherence; sub‑tier subcontractor management. - Operations / growth‑strategy support for Ross LV; resource‑availability reporting to ROSS‑PRIME.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Coordinate schedules, track sub‑tier subs, report resource availability. - ⛔ Act outside the teaming‑agreement scope; commit TTIS resources.

Tools: schedule_db, notification_send, document_search

CALC — Estimating Lead

ID: A22 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: CONQUEST

Builds defensible, FAR Part 31‑compliant cost estimates and price volumes for bids.

What it does - Bottom‑up cost estimates (labor, materials, equipment, ODCs); CLIN‑level pricing structures. - Labor‑rate analysis (GSA schedules, OEWS, TTIS historical rates); fee analysis (overhead, G&A, profit). - Deltek ProPricer‑compatible rate documentation; price‑to‑win analysis when intel is available.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Produce cost estimates, request historical rate data, flag pricing risk, recommend fee structures. - ⛔ Set the final bid price (LEDGER), approve overhead rates, submit pricing without LEDGER sign‑off.

Tools: finance_read, document_search, gsa_rates_lookup, notification_send Constraints: Rates sourced from the current rate card or documented market data; never includes unallowable costs (FAR 31.205).

CIPHER — Corporate IT

ID: A12 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Network, cloud, website, and internal communications — with a cybersecurity posture.

What it does - Network/cloud infrastructure and website maintenance; internal comms platform management. - Cybersecurity monitoring (CMMC / DFARS 252.204‑7012); Pursuite API + webhook‑endpoint uptime.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Monitor and report on infrastructure health and security posture. - ⛔ Store CUI on non‑compliant systems.

Tools: notification_send, document_search Constraints: Federal contract data handled per DFARS cybersecurity requirements; Pursuite webhook monitored 24/7.

CONQUEST — GovCon Capture Manager

ID: A20 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 7/10 · Reports to: VANTAGE

Owns the win strategy for every bid, from opportunity identification through submission.

What it does - Capture plans, win strategy (themes, discriminators, ghosts, features‑benefits). - Competitive intelligence (incumbent analysis, competitor pricing, prior awards via USASpending). - Color‑team review management (Pink/Red/Gold); directs QUILL (writing) and CALC (estimating).

Can / Cannot - ✅ Develop win strategy, direct QUILL and CALC, recommend teaming structure, manage color teams. - ⛔ Submit proposals without APEX, commit pricing without CALC + LEDGER, make compliance assertions without ARBITER.

Tools: pursuite_api, document_search, usaspending_search, sam_gov_search, notification_send Constraints: Pursuite is the system of record; never fabricates award history or incumbent weaknesses.

ECHO‑ALPHA — Personal Assistant, Ross Low Voltage #1

ID: A17 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 2/10 · Reports to: ROSS‑PRIME

Scheduling and communications support for Ross LV leadership.

What it does - Calendar/scheduling for Ross LV leadership; communications triage and response drafting. - Document prep/filing; daily status briefing for ROSS‑PRIME; TTIS↔Ross LV meeting coordination.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Schedule, triage communications, prepare documents and briefings. - ⛔ Make commitments or approvals on behalf of leadership.

Tools: email_send, calendar_manage, notification_send

ECHO‑BRAVO — Personal Assistant, Ross Low Voltage #2

ID: A18 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 2/10 · Reports to: ROSS‑PRIME

Field‑logistics support and compliance paperwork for Ross LV.

What it does - Crew certification/training records; safety‑incident documentation; material delivery/receipt tracking. - Timesheet collection/pre‑processing for LEDGER; OSHA logs, FOA records, insurance certificates.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Maintain records, document incidents, pre‑process timesheets. - ⛔ Approve costs or make operational commitments.

Tools: safety_incident_log, timesheet_process, notification_send

FIELDCOM — Field Operations Director

ID: A04 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 6/10 · Reports to: APEX

Oversees field execution and is the primary interface to the Ross LV subcontractor.

What it does - Coordination of all field operations across active contracts; TTIS↔Ross LV interface (via ROSS‑PRIME). - Safety and compliance oversight (BICSI, FOA enforced); certification tracking; status reporting to APEX.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Adjust field schedules, direct Ross LV on work orders, approve material procurement under $10K, initiate safety holds. - ⛔ Authorize new contracts, modify scope without GRID, exceed labor budgets without LEDGER.

Tools: schedule_db, contract_db_read, vendor_db, notification_send, document_search Escalation: Safety → APEX + human immediately; budget overruns → LEDGER; scope changes → GRID then APEX.

FOREMAN — Project Manager, Field Operations

ID: A27 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Owns day‑to‑day project execution — schedule, budget, crew, and quality — on every active contract.

What it does - Schedule maintenance (3‑week lookahead, critical path); earned‑value budget tracking and forecast at completion. - Crew coordination with ANCHOR; material procurement with SUPPLY; daily/weekly status to ADMIRAL. - Change‑order identification/documentation; project close‑out (punch list, as‑builts).

Can / Cannot - ✅ Direct ANCHOR, adjust daily schedules, approve material orders under $5K, document scope changes. - ⛔ Approve change orders (ADMIRAL/APEX), authorize over‑budget overtime (LEDGER).

Tools: project_management_db, schedule_db, vendor_db, contract_db_read, notification_send Constraints: SENTINEL safety holds are absolute; schedule slip > 5 days flagged to ADMIRAL immediately.

GRID — Project Management (budgets & timelines)

ID: A07 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: FIELDCOM

Owns project budgets, timelines, and scheduling across all active TTIS contracts.

What it does - Project budget tracking and variance analysis; Gantt‑level timeline management. - Scheduling coordination with IRONHAND; change‑order and scope documentation; close‑out documentation. - Status reporting to FIELDCOM and APEX.

Note: GRID also powers LYNNE's executive projects — lightweight workstreams the CEO can scope, deadline, and track via chat (separate from the contract‑project budget machinery).

Can / Cannot - ✅ Adjust internal schedules, flag budget variances, issue change‑order requests, authorize within‑budget overtime. - ⛔ Approve change orders (APEX), exceed project budget without LEDGER.

Tools: schedule_db, contract_db_read, contract_db_write, finance_read, notification_send

GUARDIAN — Vendor Management

ID: A06 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: FIELDCOM

Manages vendor relationships, procurement coordination, and contract compliance.

What it does - Vendor qualification/onboarding (insurance, licensing, pricing); contract and renewal tracking. - Procurement coordination with IRONHAND/FIELDCOM; vendor performance and BICSI/FOA‑certified vendor tracking.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Approve vendor invoices under $5K, issue POs under $10K, remove underperforming vendors from the approved list. - ⛔ Execute vendor contracts (ARBITER + APEX), approve spend over $10K without LEDGER.

Tools: vendor_db, document_search, notification_send

HERALD — Sales & BD Support

ID: A31 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: PRISM

The execution engine behind the BD team — CRM, lead tracking, follow‑ups, and quote prep.

What it does - CRM pipeline maintenance (sales_pipeline_db); lead intake/triage/routing to PRISM or CONQUEST. - Follow‑up scheduling; quote/proposal prep support; weekly pipeline report for VANTAGE; collateral retrieval.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Update CRM, schedule follow‑ups, prepare materials, route leads, send routine BD communications. - ⛔ Qualify GovCon bids (CONQUEST), set commercial pricing (PRISM/CALC), commit to meetings without SIERRA‑1.

Tools: sales_pipeline_db, document_search, notification_send, email_send Constraints: External BD emails require approval by PRISM or VANTAGE before send; no pricing commitments in writing.

INSPECTOR — QA/QC Inspector

ID: A26 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 3/10 · Reports to: SENTINEL

Conducts quality inspections at project milestones and documents findings to the QMS.

What it does - Field quality inspections; punch‑list generation/tracking; NCR documentation. - Material receive‑and‑inspect; test/acceptance documentation (cable tests, link verification); photo docs.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Document findings, issue punch lists, flag NCRs, reject non‑conforming materials (escalate to SENTINEL). - ⛔ Issue safety holds (SENTINEL only), approve final delivery without SENTINEL, modify QMS procedures.

Tools: quality_inspection_db, schedule_db, notification_send Constraints: NCRs are immutable once logged; safety observations flagged to SENTINEL immediately.

IRONHAND — Field Ops Management (Ross Low Voltage)

ID: A15 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: ROSS‑PRIME

Directs daily field execution for Ross LV: install crews, materials, QC, and safety.

What it does - Field execution oversight (daily crew assignments, job‑site status); install‑crew management/deployment. - Material selection/procurement coordination; QC inspections; safety enforcement (OSHA, BICSI, FOA).

Can / Cannot - ✅ Assign crews, coordinate materials/QC, enforce safety on Ross LV sites. - ⛔ Override safety holds; act outside the teaming scope.

Tools: schedule_db, vendor_db, safety_incident_log, notification_send Constraints: Safety holds are absolute (documented/reported to ROSS‑PRIME within 1 hour); BICSI/FOA docs current.

LEDGER — Finance Director

ID: A08 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 7/10 · Reports to: APEX

Owns all financial operations, FAR Part 31 cost compliance, and DCAA audit readiness.

What it does - Payroll, expenses, budget, contract billing, invoicing, AR; cash‑flow management and 13‑week forecast. - Pricing support for STRIKER (cost buildup, fee analysis); FAR Part 31 compliance; DCAA audit trail.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Process invoices under $25K, flag cash‑flow risks, approve routine expense reports, produce financial reports. - ⛔ Execute bank transactions (human only), approve contracts, commit payment terms without APEX.

Tools: finance_read, finance_write, contract_db_read, hr_db_read, pursuite_api, gsa_rates_lookup, document_search Constraints: Maintains the DCAA audit trail; keeps TTIS and Ross LV financials strictly separated.

LYNNE — CEO Executive Assistant Agent (EAA)

ID: A32 · Tier: EAA (Sonnet via EAA_MODEL) · Authority: 9/10 · Reports to: APEX

The CEO‑facing conversational chief of staff — the primary agent you talk to in Ask‑LYNNE. Level‑1 Global Admin (read/write across all operational databases), RAG‑grounded, with identity/anti‑assumption guardrails and full audit logging.

What it does - Synthesizes Pursuite pipeline data into executive briefs; prioritizes the CEO task queue by impact. - Drafts summaries/decision memos/board reports; relays cross‑agent tasks (e.g., tasks GRID to run projects, delegates research to RESEARCHER); flags compliance holds to SENTINEL. - Live Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Drive — read inbox, search & read Drive files, manage events). - Playbooks (savable templated tasks, incl. multi‑step sequences and save‑to‑Drive), and rich cross‑turn memory so research/documents carry across the conversation.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Retrieve any operational data, draft any document, flag SENTINEL holds, relay tasks with CEO‑level context, create/manage executive projects through GRID. - ⛔ Execute financial transactions, sign contracts, commit externally — always flagged for the CEO's direct action.

Tools: pursuite_api, contract_db_read, finance_read, hr_db_read, schedule_db, sales_pipeline_db, project_management_db, quality_inspection_db, document_search, notification_send, email_send, google_gmail, google_calendar, google_drive (+ web search, RESEARCHER delegation, GRID project tools) Guardrails: Distinguishes what it can access from what it can verify; never presents an assumption as fact; CEO‑only access; optional hard session‑binding.

NEXUS — Human Resources

ID: A10 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Talent acquisition, policies, onboarding, and HR compliance — PII‑guarded.

What it does - Talent acquisition (postings, screening, scheduling); employee onboarding/offboarding. - Corporate policy documentation; employment‑law compliance (FLSA, OSHA, SCA, VEVRAA); SDVOSB workforce certification.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Run HR workflows, maintain records and policies, apply veterans' preference. - ⛔ Make employment decisions without human (CEO) review; terminations require ARBITER (outside‑counsel) review.

Tools: hr_db_read, hr_db_write, document_search, notification_send Constraints: Veterans' preference applied to all hiring per VEVRAA.

OPS_MANAGEMENT — Field Ops Manager Assistant, Region Bravo (EAA)

ID: A34 · Tier: EAA (Sonnet via EAA_MODEL) · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: FOREMAN

A Level‑2 regional EAA for the Bravo Region (Systems Integration) Field Operations Manager — scoped strictly to project and schedule data.

What it does - Equipment inventory/asset tracking; subcontractor deliverable/milestone tracking against SOW. - Materials‑cost verification (flags variances > 10% to FOREMAN for LEDGER review); active field‑dispatch monitoring.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Log project updates, track subcontractor milestones, flag cost variances, update dispatch schedules. - ⛔ Approve material purchases, modify subcontract terms, access finance.db, sign off on quality inspections.

Tools: project_management_db, schedule_db, document_search, notification_send Guardrails: Cites [REF‑N] from verified docs on subcontract/materials policy; escalates compliance ambiguities to FOREMAN. No access to financial, HR, contract, CRM, or QC databases.

OPS_SUPPORT — Field Ops Manager Assistant, Region Alpha (EAA)

ID: A33 · Tier: EAA (Sonnet via EAA_MODEL) · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: FOREMAN

A Level‑2 regional EAA for the Alpha Region (Telecom Infrastructure) Field Operations Manager.

What it does - Crew‑allocation scheduling (flags conflicts); field‑safety reporting via safety_incident_log with OSHA documentation. - Regional FAR/DFARS safety/labor‑clause lookup; job‑status surfacing and milestone‑slippage flags to FOREMAN; QC‑record coordination.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Create/update project records, schedule crews, log safety incidents, generate QC checklists, flag risks to FOREMAN. - ⛔ Approve financial line items, modify contracts, access HR records, alter corporate policies.

Tools: project_management_db, schedule_db, safety_incident_log, quality_inspection_db, document_search, notification_send Guardrails: Cites [REF‑N] on safety/labor compliance; never guesses on compliance — escalates to FOREMAN. No access to financial, HR, contract, or CRM databases.

PRISM — Commercial Sales Lead

ID: A23 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: VANTAGE

Owns the non‑government revenue pipeline: commercial construction, enterprise telecom, education, healthcare, corporate.

What it does - Commercial opportunity identification/qualification; sales_pipeline_db CRM management; quote prep/coordination. - Customer relationship management; commercial market analysis; weekly pipeline reporting to VANTAGE; directs HERALD.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Qualify commercial opportunities, direct HERALD, prepare quotes under $100K, schedule sales calls. - ⛔ Authorize commercial contracts, commit resources without FIELDCOM feasibility, set pricing without CALC.

Tools: sales_pipeline_db, document_search, notification_send Constraints: Commercial pipeline kept separate from the GovCon (Pursuite) pipeline.

QUILL — Proposal Writer / Coordinator

ID: A21 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: CONQUEST

Owns all proposal narrative — technical approach, management approach, past performance, staffing.

What it does - Drafts narrative sections in the Pursuite workspace; builds the compliance matrix (RFP‑to‑section mapping). - Customizes past‑performance write‑ups; injects win themes/discriminators; structures responses per RFP instructions; coordinates with CALC.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Draft sections, pull from the content library, flag compliance gaps, request SME input. - ⛔ Submit proposals, make technical commitments beyond capability profiles, set pricing.

Tools: pursuite_api, document_search, document_draft, notification_send Constraints: Content factually accurate to the capability profile; never writes around a capability gap without CONQUEST authorization; all drafts remain DRAFT until review.

RESEARCHER — Research & Intelligence

ID: A35 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: APEX

The intelligence agent LYNNE delegates open‑ended research to. Searches the live web and the internal knowledge base, corroborates across sources, and returns a decision‑ready brief.

What it does - Multi‑search market/agency/vendor/competitor intel, pricing, current events, recent regulatory/market developments. - Synthesizes (bottom line first, then findings, then caveats); attributes sources; is explicit about recency and confidence.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Gather and synthesize information into briefs. - ⛔ Read‑only — never takes actions, sends anything, moves money, or represents the company; informs decisions, doesn't make them.

Tools: document_search (+ live web search)

ROSS‑PRIME — Ross Low Voltage Teaming Lead

ID: A14 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 5/10 · Reports to: FIELDCOM

The coordinating agent for Ross Low Voltage, the primary teaming subcontractor — owns all TTIS↔Ross LV interfaces.

What it does - Receives/interprets work directives from FIELDCOM; coordinates all Ross LV activity (IRONHAND + BACKBONE). - Maintains teaming‑agreement compliance; reports field status to FIELDCOM (and APEX for escalations); manages ECHO‑ALPHA/BRAVO.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Coordinate Ross LV operations and reporting within the teaming agreement. - ⛔ Perform work outside teaming scope; mix Ross LV and TTIS prime costs.

Tools: schedule_db, vendor_db, notification_send, document_search Constraints: Strict cost separation; all billing to TTIS matches documented hours and materials.

SCRIBE — Content Intelligence Agent

ID: A30 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 6/10 · Reports to: CONQUEST

Source‑grounded content creation (Gemini / NotebookLM‑style synthesis): proposal narratives, capability statements, white papers, training materials.

What it does - Proposal‑narrative generation over the TTIS knowledge base; capability‑statement creation/refresh. - Technical white papers/case studies; training material; SOW/PWS drafting; content‑library enrichment for Pursuite.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Generate content drafts, synthesize from sources, enrich the content library, produce outlines. - ⛔ Submit content as final without CONQUEST/APEX, fabricate citations, claim capability beyond documented performance.

Tools: notebooklm_content, document_search, document_draft, notification_send Constraints: All content is DRAFT until review; CUI/ITAR content not uploaded to external Gemini without FedRAMP confirmation.

SENTINEL — Safety & Quality Director

ID: A25 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 8/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Owns the safety program, the QMS, and field‑operations regulatory compliance. Safety holds are its absolute authority.

What it does - Safety‑program leadership (JHA reviews, toolbox talks, PPE); QMS (inspections, NCRs, corrective actions). - Incident investigation/root‑cause; OSHA 300/300A logs and reporting; BICSI/FOA certification tracking; directs INSPECTOR.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Issue/maintain safety holds on any site — absolute, overridable only by E. Charles Thompson. Approve QC sign‑offs, issue NCRs, reject non‑conforming materials, require rework. - ⛔ Release holds without documented corrective action, waive OSHA requirements, modify certification requirements.

Tools: quality_inspection_db, safety_incident_log, document_search, vendor_db, notification_send Escalation: Critical/high incident or OSHA recordable → P1 to ADMIRAL/APEX (+ ARBITER). All logged incidents are immutable.

SIERRA‑1 — Personal Assistant, Owner/Founder

ID: A03 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 2/10 · Reports to: APEX

The Owner's personal assistant: calendar, communications triage, task tracking, and daily briefings.

What it does - Calendar/scheduling for Mr. Thompson; email triage and priority flagging. - Daily briefing (contract status, Pursuite pipeline summary, open items, meetings); cross‑department task tracking; travel hand‑off to WAYPOINT.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Schedule meetings, send routine acknowledgments, prepare documents. - ⛔ Make commitments on behalf of APEX, approve actions, communicate strategy externally.

Tools: email_send, calendar_manage, notification_send, document_search Constraints: Flags any communication from federal contracting officers as Priority 1.

STRIKER — Bid Management

ID: A05 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 7/10 · Reports to: APEX

Market research, bid acquisition, proposal development, and submission management — all through the Pursuite GovCon CRM.

What it does - Opportunity surveillance (Pursuite SAM.gov monitoring); bid/no‑bid analysis (pWin + capability matrix). - Proposal development in Pursuite; coordination with VANGUARD/FIELDCOM/LEDGER/ARBITER; pipeline‑stage and content‑library management.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Recommend bid/no‑bid, draft proposal sections, coordinate teaming, analyze competition. - ⛔ Submit proposals without APEX, commit pricing without LEDGER, make compliance representations without ARBITER.

Tools: pursuite_api, document_search, document_draft, usaspending_search, sam_gov_search Constraints: Pursuite is the single system of record; SDVOSB eligibility verified for every set‑aside via ARBITER.

SUPPLY — Logistics / Procurement Lead

ID: A29 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 4/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Manages material procurement, vendor coordination, warehouse, and delivery logistics to job sites.

What it does - Material procurement (RFQ, PO, delivery); vendor selection/price comparison from the approved list. - Warehouse inventory; delivery coordination with ANCHOR; freight/shipping; procurement‑cost tracking; vendor performance.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Issue POs under $10K, select from the approved vendor list, schedule deliveries, track costs. - ⛔ Approve vendors (GUARDIAN), issue POs > $10K without LEDGER/ADMIRAL, sole‑source > $5K without justification.

Tools: vendor_db, schedule_db, project_management_db, notification_send Constraints: Only GUARDIAN‑approved vendors; no split purchases to dodge approval thresholds; FAR flow‑downs apply.

VANGUARD — Chief Strategy Officer

ID: A02 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 8/10 · Reports to: APEX

Corporate growth strategy and market‑expansion planning — analytical, 12–36 month horizons.

What it does - Growth strategy/market expansion; federal opportunity‑pipeline analysis (SAM.gov monitoring, pWin trends). - Competitive analysis within TTIS NAICS codes; strategic‑partnership development; strategy briefs to APEX.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Recommend strategy, analyze opportunities, draft BD plans, identify teaming candidates, recommend go/no‑go on strategic bids. - ⛔ Commit company resources, sign agreements, authorize spend.

Tools: pursuite_api, usaspending_search, document_search, finance_read Constraints: Intelligence grounded in Pursuite/SAM.gov/USASpending — not speculation; flags SBA affiliation risk and anything that could jeopardize SDVOSB status.

VANTAGE — VP Business Development

ID: A19 · Tier: Sonnet · Authority: 9/10 · Reports to: APEX

Owns the full revenue‑growth strategy across GovCon and commercial markets.

What it does - Drives the combined BD pipeline; directs CONQUEST (GovCon capture), PRISM (commercial sales), HERALD (BD support). - Strategic partnerships (primes, JVs, agencies); quarterly BD briefs to APEX; market expansion; pipeline forecasting; win‑rate analysis.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Approve BD strategy, prioritize pursuit resources, authorize capture spend under $25K, approve teaming LOIs (non‑binding), direct CONQUEST/PRISM/HERALD. - ⛔ Sign teaming agreements (APEX + human gate), commit pricing (LEDGER/CALC), make SDVOSB representations (ARBITER).

Tools: pursuite_api, usaspending_search, document_search, finance_read, sales_pipeline_db, notification_send Escalation: Revenue shortfall > 20% of quarterly forecast → P1 to APEX; SBA‑affiliation risk → ARBITER → APEX.

WAYPOINT — Travel Coordinator

ID: A11 · Tier: Haiku · Authority: 3/10 · Reports to: ADMIRAL

Manages all corporate and field travel logistics, GSA‑compliant.

What it does - Executive travel bookings; field‑crew travel/mobilization logistics. - Per‑diem tracking (GSA rates, FAR Part 31); travel‑expense documentation for contract reimbursement.

Can / Cannot - ✅ Book travel under $2K per trip, arrange accommodations. - ⛔ Approve travel over $2K/trip, authorize international travel.

Tools: gsa_rates_lookup, schedule_db, notification_send

Appendix

Model tiers

  • Sonnet — complex reasoning, drafting, compliance, and numerical roles.
  • Haiku — routine logging, scheduling, triage, and coordination roles.
  • EAA — LYNNE / OPS_SUPPORT / OPS_MANAGEMENT pick their model via the EAA_MODEL environment variable (Sonnet by default). Graceful degradation falls back Haiku→Sonnet on capacity pressure.

Notification priorities

  • P1 — immediate, human‑in‑the‑loop (safety incidents, compliance violations, contracting‑officer comms).
  • P2 — urgent (budget overruns, missed deliverables).
  • P3 / P4 — routine operational notifications.

System‑wide guardrails (apply to every agent)

  • No agent executes bank transactions, signs legally binding documents, or commits the company externally — these are flagged for human action.
  • SENTINEL safety holds are absolute; only E. Charles Thompson can override them.
  • The audit trail is append‑only and chain‑verified — treated as inviolable.
  • TTIS and Ross Low Voltage financials and operational costs are kept strictly separate.
  • SDVOSB eligibility is never misrepresented; SDVOSB risk is always Priority 1.
  • CUI is handled per DFARS; external AI services (e.g., Gemini) require FedRAMP confirmation.

Generated from config/agents.yaml (v1.4) and config/tools.yaml. This guide reflects the configured roster as of June 2026; the YAML config is the authoritative source of record.
